query: brachytherapy point dose measurement verification thermoluminescent dosimetry TLD
citations: [Point-A vs. volume-based brachytherapy for the treatment of cervix cancer: A meta-analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35259419/)
[American Brachytherapy Society consensus guidelines for adjuvant vaginal cuff brachytherapy after hysterectomy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22265439/)
[Point: brachytherapy versus intensity-modulated radiation therapy in the management of base of tongue cancers.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15737897/)
[Transition from point A to volume based image guided brachytherapy across a network of centers in India through workshop and mentoring.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39721875/)

evidence.claim:
PubMed ID: 35259419. 2022/05/01 00:00. PURPOSE: To report disease-free survival (DFS) for volume-based and 
point-A based brachytherapy (BT) in locally advanced cervical cancer.
MATERIALS & METHODS: W
evidence.source_title: Point-A vs. volume-based brachytherapy for the treatment of cervix cancer: A meta-analysis.
evidence.raw_snippet:
PubMed ID: 35259419. 2022/05/01 00:00. PURPOSE: To report disease-free survival (DFS) for volume-based and 
point-A based brachytherapy (BT) in locally advanced cervical cancer.
MATERIALS & METHODS: We conducted a meta-analysis of studies assessing the 
effects of point-A and volume-based brachytherapy on 3-year DFS. Studies 
including stage I-IVA cervical cancer patients were included if standard 
treatment of concomitant chemo-radiotherapy and high-dose- or pulsed dose rate 
BT was delivered.
